---
title: "nitrix.geometry.pyramid"
generated: true
signature-hash: ced0b72d17c67488
description: "Multi-resolution Gaussian pyramid."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-pyramid-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-pyramid/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.pyramid`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Multi-resolution Gaussian pyramid.

Coarse-to-fine registration warm-starts each resolution from the
solution of the coarser one above it.  [`gaussian_pyramid`](nitrix-geometry-pyramid/gaussian_pyramid.qmd) builds the
image stack; [`downsample`](nitrix-geometry-pyramid/downsample.qmd) and [`upsample`](nitrix-geometry-pyramid/upsample.qmd) are the single-step
anti-aliased resize and the (interpolating) prolongation.

Composition only -- no new kernel: [`downsample`](nitrix-geometry-pyramid/downsample.qmd) is
`gaussian` (anti-alias) followed by [`resample`](nitrix-geometry-grid/resample.qmd)
(align-corners resize); [`upsample`](nitrix-geometry-pyramid/upsample.qmd) is [`resample`](nitrix-geometry-grid/resample.qmd) to a
larger shape.  Channel-last image layout `(*spatial, c)` matches
[`resample`](nitrix-geometry-grid/resample.qmd) and [`spatial_transform`](nitrix-geometry-grid/spatial_transform.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `c` | feature channels (channels-last) |
| `*target` | the target grid's spatial axes (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-pyramid-symbols}
:::
